text.title: strategy-analysis-business-need Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for strategy-analysis-business-need. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The business need statement MUST be outcome-shaped, not solution-shaped. Any statement containing "implement", "deploy", "build", "buy", or "migrate" MUST be recast as a measurable outcome before proceeding.
rule.rationale: Solution-shaped needs hard-code a choice before the problem is understood. The BA and agents must refuse them because they collapse the solution space before the gap analysis has been done.

rule.statement: Every driver MUST cite a primary-source verbatim quote with document name and date. Drivers without evidence MUST be flagged as unverified and excluded from the signed need statement.
rule.rationale: Generic driver prose ("AI is disrupting industries") is ignored in steering reviews. Evidence-backed drivers survive scrutiny and give the BA standing to defend the need.

rule.statement: The consequence of inaction MUST be falsifiable and quantified where possible. Aspirational language ("things will deteriorate") MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a measurable consequence of inaction, leadership cannot see the cost of doing nothing; the strategy reads as advocacy rather than analysis.

rule.statement: A named decision-maker MUST be identified before need-framing is complete. If no decision-maker is named, the need frame is not signed off; downstream agents MUST NOT begin current-state work.
rule.rationale: Without a decision-maker, change strategy recommendations have no authority; the analysis floats until someone with budget and mandate takes ownership.
